description_long: This is a vintage-style, single-engine airplane with a high-mounted straight wing configuration and a fixed taildragger landing gear. The fuselage is painted green, while the wings are primarily gray with red leading edges, and the tail section is white. The engine is mounted at the nose, featuring a visible propeller and exposed engine components. The registration number 'GU8338' is clearly marked on the upper surface of the right wing. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage and appears to be enclosed, with a small window visible. The overall design suggests a classic utility or transport aircraft, likely constructed from metal and featuring riveted panels.
description: A classic green and gray high-wing airplane with a nose-mounted propeller, red wing accents, and a visible registration number on the wing.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the nose-mounted propeller, exposed engine, and high-wing configuration with red leading edges.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the tail section, white tailplane, and the underside of the gray wings.
description_view_2: Rear-right view displaying the registration number 'GU8338' on the right wing and the green fuselage.
description_view_3: Front-right view focusing on the propeller, engine details, and the cockpit area at the front of the fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with exposed components and a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single window, likely single or dual seat.
